backgrounds.name: Noble
backgrounds.description: You understand wealth, power, and privilege. You carry a noble title, and your family owns land, collects taxes, and wields significant political influence. You might be a pampered aristocrat unfamiliar with hardship, or a disgraced noble driven to prove your worth. Whatever your circumstances, your birthright shapes how the world sees you—and how you see it.

features.name: Position of Privilege
features.desc: Thanks to your noble birth, people are inclined to think the best of you. You are welcome in high society, and common folk make every effort to accommodate you and avoid your displeasure. Other people of high birth treat you as a member of the same social sphere. You can secure an audience with a local noble if you need to.

from.name: Knight
from.desc: You are a noble who serves as a knight, sworn to a liege lord or order. You have retainers who attend to your needs and carry messages.

choices.prompt: Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: My eloquent flattery makes everyone I talk to feel like the most wonderful and important person in the world.
The common folk love me for my kindness and generosity.
No one could doubt by looking at my regal bearing that I am a cut above the unwashed masses.
I take great pains to always look my best and follow the latest fashions.
I don't like to get my hands dirty, and I won't be caught dead in unsuitable accommodations.
Despite my noble birth, I do not place myself above other folk. We all have the same blood.
My favor, once lost, is lost forever.
If you do me an injury, I will crush you, ruin your name, and salt your fields.

from.name: Respect
from.desc: Respect is due to me because of my position, but all people deserve to be treated with dignity. (Good)

from.name: Responsibility
from.desc: It is my duty to respect the authority of those above me, just as those below me must respect mine. (Lawful)

from.name: Independence
from.desc: I must prove that I can handle myself without the coddling of my family. (Chaotic)

from.name: Power
from.desc: If I can attain more power, no one will tell me what to do. (Evil)

from.name: Family
from.desc: Blood runs thicker than water. (Any)

from.name: Noble Obligation
from.desc: It is my duty to protect and care for the people beneath me. (Good)

from.text: I will face any challenge to win the approval of my family.
My house's alliance with another noble family must be sustained at all costs.
Nothing is more important than the other members of my family.
I am in love with the heir of a family that my family despises.
My loyalty to my sovereign is unwavering.
The common folk must see me as a hero of the people.
I secretly believe that everyone is beneath me.
I hide a truly scandalous secret that could ruin my family forever.
I too often hear veiled insults and threats in every word addressed to me.
I have an insatiable desire for carnal pleasures.
In fact, the world does revolve around me.
By my words and actions, I often bring shame to my family.

backgrounds.name: Outlander
backgrounds.description: You grew up in the wilds, far from civilization and the comforts of town and technology. You've witnessed the migration of herds larger than forests, survived weather more extreme than any city-dweller could comprehend, and enjoyed the solitude of being the only thinking creature for miles in any direction. The wilds are in your blood, whether you were a nomad, an explorer, a recluse, a hunter-gatherer, or even a marauder. Even in places where you don't know the specific features of the terrain, you know the ways of the wild.

equipment: A trophy from an animal you killed

features.name: Wanderer
features.desc: You have an excellent memory for maps and geography, and you can always recall the general layout of terrain, settlements, and other features around you. In addition, you can find food and fresh water for yourself and up to five other people each day, provided that the land offers berries, small game, water, and so forth.

choices.prompt: Choose your origin as an outlander (or roll)
Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: I’m driven by a wanderlust that led me away from home.
I watch over my friends as if they were a litter of newborn pups.
I once ran twenty-five miles without stopping to warn my clan of an approaching orc horde. I'd do it again if I had to.
I have a lesson for every situation, drawn from observing nature.
I place no stock in wealthy or well-mannered folk. Money and manners won’t save you from a hungry owlbear.
I’m always picking things up, absently fiddling with them, and sometimes accidentally breaking them.
I feel far more comfortable around animals than people.
I was, in fact, raised by wolves.

from.name: Change
from.desc: Life is like the seasons, in constant change, and we must change with it. (Chaotic)

from.name: Greater Good
from.desc: It is each person’s responsibility to make the most happiness for the whole tribe. (Good)

from.name: Honor
from.desc: If I dishonor myself, I dishonor my whole clan. (Lawful)

from.name: Might
from.desc: The strongest are meant to rule. (Evil)

from.name: Nature
from.desc: The natural world is more important than all the constructs of civilization. (Neutral)

from.name: Glory
from.desc: I must earn glory in battle, for myself and my clan. (Any)

from.text: My family, clan, or tribe is the most important thing in my life, even when they are far from me.
An injury to the unspoiled wilderness of my home is an injury to me.
I will bring terrible wrath down on the evildoers who destroyed my homeland.
I am the last of my tribe, and it is up to me to ensure their names enter legend.
I suffer awful visions of a coming disaster and will do anything to prevent it.
It is my duty to provide children to sustain my tribe.
I am too enamored of ale, wine, and other intoxicants.
There’s no room for caution in a life lived to the fullest.
I remember every insult I’ve received and nurse a silent resentment toward anyone who’s ever wronged me.
I am slow to trust members of other races, tribes, and societies.
Violence is my answer to almost any challenge.
Don’t expect me to save those who can’t save themselves. It is nature’s way that the strong thrive and the weak perish.

backgrounds.name: Rewarded
backgrounds.description:
You were living a difficult life before your destiny suddenly changed through a miraculous turn of good fortune. Perhaps a benevolent deity gave you precisely what you most desired. You might have stumbled across a Deck of Many Things and drawn a card with a potent, positive effect. Or maybe you inherited a modest fortune from a distant relative you didn’t know you had.

Regardless, you left the daily miseries of your old life behind in favor of a life of adventure and excitement. Your old debts have been paid, responsibilities you thought inescapable are behind you, or you suddenly possess rare and unusual skills unknown to ordinary folk.

equipment: A gaming set (matching your chosen proficiency)

features.name: Fortune’s Favor
features.desc: Your unexpected good fortune is reflected by a minor boon. You gain the Lucky, Magic Initiate, or Skilled feat (your choice). Your choice of feat reflects the transformation that changed your life.

choices.prompt: Choose the feat that represents your good fortune
Choose 1 personality trait (or write your own)

from.text: A safe home is a foundation on which anything else can be built. (Key, Throne)
I was elevated to heights I could never otherwise attain, and I won’t waste my fortune. (Star, Sun)
I try to be a source of inspiration and joy to others. Life is never as bad as you think! (Euryale, Jester)
Courage and boldness can carry the day when all else fails. (Comet, Knight)
My good fortune means I can lift others up as well. (Gem, Moon)
Having the right answers is the first step to solving any problem, no matter how dire. (Fates, Sage)

choices.prompt: Choose or roll a rewarded trinket

from.text: A perfumed silk scarf from an admirer
A crystal bead that glows like a candle in the dark
A letter of introduction and invitation from an influential person in a far-off city
A motto or symbol whose meaning eludes you, etched on a piece of your equipment
A crisp playing card that never seems to tear or soil, depicting the card that affected you
Half a medallion designed to be rejoined to its other half

backgrounds.name: Ruined
backgrounds.description:
Everything was going so well! You had a life of luxury, love, and comfort when you suddenly lost it all. Perhaps you were framed for crimes you didn’t commit and lost your reputation, fortune, and career. Maybe a rampaging dragon or another monster wiped out everything you had in a single calamitous afternoon. Or you might have sought out a Deck of Many Things, hoping to make your successful life even more glorious—only to draw a destructive card that changed your destiny forever.

Your desperation has driven you to the career of adventurer. You don’t seek out dark dungeons and their monstrous inhabitants for excitement and glory; you do it because every other path is closed to you. But you have risen high on fortune’s wheel once before, and with luck and fortitude, you could do so again.

equipment: A gaming set (matching your chosen proficiency)

features.name: Still Standing
features.desc: You have weathered ruinous misfortune, and you possess hidden reserves others don’t expect. You gain the Alert, Skilled, or Tough feat (your choice). Your choice of feat reflects how you’ve dealt with the terrible loss that changed your life forever. If you’ve kept your senses sharp for every opportunity and climbed your way out of misery by seizing the tiniest scrap of hope, choose Alert. If you’ve redoubled your efforts to reclaim what was once yours, choose Skilled. If you’ve stoically persevered through your misfortune, select Tough.

choices.prompt: Choose the feat that represents how you survived your ruin
Choose 1 personality trait (or write your own)

from.text: I’ve changed from my past, and I work to live up to my new path. (Balance, Throne)
Every moment is a gift I refuse to squander. (Euryale, Skull)
Now that I’ve overcome having nothing, I can survive anything. (Fool, Ruin, Talons)
I know enemies are set against me, and I always prepare for the worst. (Flames, Rogue)
I interpret every event as part of a larger pattern I just haven’t worked out yet. (Puzzle, Star)
I must make up for so much time I’ve already lost. (Donjon, Void)

choices.prompt: Choose or roll a ruined trinket

from.text: A rusted scrap of a once-beloved family heirloom
A land deed, but all the names and markings that once tied it to you have faded into obscurity
A bauble once imbued with powerful magic
A battered playing card with a hole pierced in it, its face depicting the card that affected you
A yellowed Humanoid tooth that whispers eerily when placed under a pillow
A keepsake from someone you were once close to but who is now your enemy

backgrounds.name: Rune Carver

source.book: Bigby Presents: Glory of the Giants

backgrounds.description:
You’ve dedicated your life to studying runecraft. Whether you were taught by a master rune carver or learned by poring over ancient engravings, you can tap into the supernatural power held within runes.

The art of runecraft was initially created by giants, but over time, the practice has been adopted by many peoples. As such, though Giant runes are the most commonly used, many rune carvers incorporate symbols from their native languages into the craft.

equipment: A set of artisan’s tools (one of your choice)

features.name: Rune Shaper
features.desc: You gain the Rune Shaper feat.

choices.prompt: Choose or roll a rune style

from.text: You inscribe runes in wax or clay with a fine metal needle.
You whittle pieces of wood into small figurines you mark with runes.
You engrave runes onto glass beads and thread them onto necklaces and bracelets.
You stitch your runes into the hems of clothing.
You carve runes on a set of animal bones you can throw in different formations.
You draw your runes into candles, melting the wax to smooth over the engravings.

choices.prompt: Choose 1 personality trait (or write your own)

from.text: Is it practical to learn an ancient language that is rarely spoken? No. But is it fun? Very.
I learned one of my ancestors was a lauded rune carver whose story was lost to time. I seek to rekindle that legacy.
The old, traditional markings of runecraft look so boring. Why not give my runes some flair?
In my studies of runes, I strive to understand how great civilizations of the past fell, so that I may prevent it from happening to societies of the present.
Life may be a whirlwind of chaos around me, but whenever I create my runes, I feel at peace.
My brain struggles to process ink words written on paper, but the tactile feeling of carved runes makes my mind sing.

backgrounds.name: Sage
backgrounds.description: You spent years learning the lore of the multiverse. You scoured manuscripts, studied scrolls, and listened to the greatest experts on the subjects that interest you. Your efforts have made you a master in your fields of study.

equipment: A letter from a dead colleague posing a question you have not yet been able to answer

features.name: Researcher
features.desc: When you attempt to learn or recall a piece of lore, if you do not know that information, you often know where and from whom you can obtain it. Usually, this information comes from a library, scriptorium, university, or a sage or other learned person or creature. Your DM might rule that the knowledge you seek is secreted away in an inaccessible place, or that it simply cannot be found.

choices.prompt: Choose or roll a scholarly specialty
Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: I use polysyllabic words that convey the impression of great erudition.
I've read every book in the world's greatest libraries—or I like to boast that I have.
I'm used to helping out those who aren't as smart as I am, and I patiently explain anything and everything to others.
There's nothing I like more than a good mystery.
I'm willing to listen to every side of an argument before I make my own judgment.
I... speak... slowly... when talking... to idiots... which... almost... everyone... is... compared... to me.
I am horribly, horribly awkward in social situations.
I'm convinced that people are always trying to steal my secrets.

from.name: Knowledge
from.desc: The path to power and self-improvement is through knowledge. (Neutral)

from.name: Beauty
from.desc: What is beautiful points us beyond itself toward what is true. (Good)

from.name: Logic
from.desc: Emotions must not cloud our logical thinking. (Lawful)

from.name: No Limits
from.desc: Nothing should fetter the infinite possibility inherent in all existence. (Chaotic)

from.name: Power
from.desc: Knowledge is the path to power and domination. (Evil)

from.name: Self-Improvement
from.desc: The goal of a life of study is the betterment of oneself. (Any)

from.text: It is my duty to protect my students.
I have an ancient text that holds terrible secrets that must not fall into the wrong hands.
I work to preserve a library, university, scriptorium, or monastery.
My life's work is a series of tomes related to a specific field of lore.
I've been searching my whole life for the answer to a certain question.
I sold my soul for knowledge. I hope to do great deeds and win it back.
I am easily distracted by the promise of information.
Most people scream and run when they see a demon. I stop and take notes on its anatomy.
Unlocking an ancient mystery is worth the price of a civilization.
I overlook obvious solutions in favor of complicated ones.
I speak without really thinking through my words, invariably insulting others.
I can’t keep a secret to save my life, or anyone else’s.

backgrounds.name: Sailor
backgrounds.description: You sailed on a seagoing vessel for years. In that time, you faced down mighty storms, monsters of the deep, and those who wanted to sink your craft to the bottomless depths. Your first love is the distant line of the horizon, but the time has come to try your hand at something new.

variants.name: Pirate
variants.description: You spent your youth under the sway of a black flag and a cutlass, raiding merchant ships and coastal settlements. Fear and violence were your stock in trade, and you reveled in your infamy.

equipment: A lucky charm (rabbit foot or small stone)

features.name: Ship's Passage
features.desc: When you need to, you can secure free passage on a sailing ship for yourself and your adventuring companions. You might sail on the ship you served on, or another ship you have good relations with. Because you're calling in a favor, you can't be certain of a schedule or route that will meet your every need. Your DM will determine how long it takes to get where you're going.

choices.prompt: Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: My friends know they can rely on me, no matter what.
I work hard so that I can play hard when the work is done.
I enjoy sailing into new ports and making new friends over a flagon of ale.
I stretch the truth for the sake of a good story.
To me, a tavern brawl is a nice way to get to know a new city.
I never pass up a friendly wager.
My language is as foul as an otyugh nest.
I like a job well done, especially if I can convince someone else to do it.

from.name: Respect
from.desc: The thing that keeps a ship together is mutual respect between captain and crew. (Good)

from.name: Fairness
from.desc: We all do the work, so we all share in the rewards. (Lawful)

from.name: Freedom
from.desc: The sea is freedom—the freedom to go anywhere and do anything. (Chaotic)

from.name: Mastery
from.desc: I'm a predator, and the other ships on the sea are my prey. (Evil)

from.name: People
from.desc: I'm committed to my crewmates, not to ideals. (Neutral)

from.name: Aspiration
from.desc: Someday I'll own my own ship and chart my own destiny. (Any)

from.text: I'm loyal to my captain first, everything else second.
The ship is most important—crewmates and captains come and go.
I'll always remember my first ship.
In a harbor town, I have a paramour whose eyes nearly stole me from the sea.
I was cheated out of my fair share of the profits, and I want revenge.
Rumor has it that a mythical treasure lies at the end of a particular sea route.
I follow orders, even if I think they're wrong.
I'll say anything to avoid having to do extra work.
Once someone questions my courage, I never back down no matter how dangerous the situation.
Once I start drinking, it's hard for me to stop.
I can't help but pocket loose coins and other trinkets I come across.
My pride will probably lead to my destruction.

backgrounds.name: Shipwright
backgrounds.description: You have sailed into war on the decks of great ships, patching their hulls with soup bowls and prayers. You once helped build a fishing vessel that single-handedly saved a town from starvation. You have seen a majestic prow in your dreams that you have not been able to replicate in wood. Since childhood, you have loved the water and have been captivated by the many vessels that travel on it.

equipment: A set of well-loved carpenter's tools

features.name: I’ll Patch It!
features.desc: Provided you have carpenter’s tools and wood, you can perform repairs on a water vehicle. When you use this ability, you restore a number of hit points to the hull of a water vehicle equal to 5 × your proficiency bonus. A vehicle can’t be patched by you in this way again until after it has been pulled ashore and fully repaired.

choices.prompt: Choose or roll for your life at sea influence

from.name: Grand Designs
from.desc: You are working on plans and schematics for a new, very fast ship and seek to study many kinds of vessels.

from.name: Solid and Sound
from.desc: You saved a war galley from sinking; the local navy regards you as a friend.

from.name: Favored
from.desc: Your insistence on better construction saved a merchant ship; you have an open invitation to the merchant’s mansion.

from.name: Master of Armaments
from.desc: You specialized in designing and mounting naval defenses and can judge their quality at a glance.

from.name: Low Places
from.desc: You have contacts among smugglers along the coast who trade coin and favors for repairs.

from.name: Mysteries of the Deep
from.desc: You encountered a possibly divine being at sea who revealed a secret of the deep to you.

choices.prompt: Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: I love talking and being heard more than I like to listen.
I'm extremely fond of puzzles.
I thrive under pressure.
I love sketching and designing objects, especially boats.
I'm not afraid of hard work—in fact, I prefer it.
A pipe, an ale, and the smell of the sea: paradise.
I have an endless supply of cautionary tales about the sea.
I don't mind getting my hands dirty.

from.name: Crew
from.desc: If everyone on deck pitches in, we’ll never sink. (Good)

from.name: Careful Lines
from.desc: A ship must be balanced according to the laws of the universe. (Lawful)

from.name: Invention
from.desc: Make what you need out of whatever is at hand. (Chaotic)

from.name: Perfection
from.desc: To measure a being and find it lacking is the greatest disappointment. (Evil)

from.name: Reflection
from.desc: Muddied water always clears in time. (Any)

from.name: Hope
from.desc: The horizon at sea holds the greatest promise. (Any)

from.text: I must visit all the oceans of the world and behold the ships that sail there.
Much of the treasure I claim will be used to enrich my community.
I must find a kind of wood rumored to possess magical qualities.
I repair broken things to redeem what’s broken in myself.
I will craft a boat capable of sailing through the most dangerous storms.
A kraken destroyed my masterpiece; its teeth shall adorn my hearth.
I never know when to throw something away—it might be useful later.
Shoddy craftsmanship infuriates me.
Everything I make ends up looking like it belongs on a ship.
I forget to eat or sleep while sketching new designs.
I judge people harshly if they lack skill with tools.
I sometimes take finely made things that don’t belong to me.

backgrounds.name: Smuggler
backgrounds.description: On a rickety barge, you carried a hundred longswords in fish barrels right past the dock master’s oblivious lackeys. You paddled a riverboat filled with stolen elven wine under the gaze of the moon and sold it for twice its value by morning. In kinder moments, you’ve ferried innocents out of war zones or guided livestock to safety along burning rivers. You survive by wits, nerve, and knowing when to lie—and when not to.

equipment: A fancy leather vest or a pair of leather boots

features.name: Down Low
features.desc: You are acquainted with a network of smugglers willing to help you out of tight situations. While in a particular town, city, or similar community (DM’s discretion), you and your companions can stay for free in safe houses. Safe houses provide a poor lifestyle. While staying at a safe house, you can choose to keep your presence—and that of your companions—a secret.

choices.prompt: Choose or roll for your smuggling claim to fame

from.name: Spirit of the Whale
from.desc: You smuggled dwarven spirits inside a dead whale, which later exploded in a shower of blubber and whiskey.

from.name: Cart and Sword
from.desc: You drove stolen art through an active battlefield while singing sea shanties.

from.name: The Recruit
from.desc: You joined a foreign navy to smuggle jewels, gained rank, then vanished.

from.name: River of Shadows
from.desc: Your riverboat slipped into the Shadowfell, where you sold stolen dragonborn relics.

from.name: Gold-Hearted
from.desc: You bribed guards with all your gold to save a fleeing family.

from.name: Playing Both Sides
from.desc: You supplied both sides of a war simultaneously without being discovered.

choices.prompt: Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: I love being on the water but hate fishing.
I think of everything in terms of monetary value.
I never stop smiling.
Nothing rattles me; I have a lie for every occasion.
I love gold but won’t cheat a friend.
I enjoy doing things others think are impossible.
I become wistful when I see the sun rise over the ocean.
I am no common criminal; I am a mastermind.

from.name: Wealth
from.desc: Heaps of coins in a secure vault are all I dream of. (Any)

from.name: Smuggler’s Code
from.desc: I uphold the unwritten rules: don’t cheat fellow smugglers or harm innocents. (Lawful)

from.name: All for a Coin
from.desc: I’ll do nearly anything if it turns a profit. (Evil)

from.name: Peace and Prosperity
from.desc: I smuggle to benefit my community. (Good)

from.name: People
from.desc: For all my lies, friendship matters most. (Any)

from.name: Daring
from.desc: Risking everything makes me feel alive. (Any)

from.text: My vessel was stolen, and I will recover it.
I intend to lead the smuggling network I belong to.
I owe a debt that can’t be repaid in gold.
After one last job, I’ll retire for good.
A fellow smuggler betrayed me and stole something precious.
I secretly give most of my profits to a charitable cause.
Lying is reflexive; I sometimes do it without realizing.
I assess relationships in terms of profit and loss.
I believe everyone has a price.
I struggle to trust anyone.
Few people know the real me.
Though I act charming, I feel nothing for others.

backgrounds.name: Soldier
backgrounds.description: War has been your life for as long as you care to remember. You trained as a youth, studied the use of weapons and armor, learned basic survival techniques, and drilled endlessly until you learned how to stay alive on the battlefield. You might have been part of a standing national army or a mercenary company, or perhaps a member of a local militia who rose to prominence during a recent war.

equipment: A trophy taken from a fallen enemy
A set of bone dice or a deck of playing cards

features.name: Military Rank
features.desc: You have a military rank from your career as a soldier. Soldiers loyal to your former military organization still recognize your authority and influence, and they defer to you if they are of a lower rank. You can invoke your rank to exert influence over other soldiers and requisition simple equipment or horses for temporary use. You can also usually gain access to friendly military encampments and fortresses where your rank is recognized.

choices.prompt: Choose or roll for your military specialty
Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: I’m always polite and respectful.
I’m haunted by memories of war. I can’t get the images of violence out of my mind.
I’ve lost too many friends, and I’m slow to make new ones.
I’m full of inspiring and cautionary tales from my military experience.
I can stare down a hell hound without flinching.
I enjoy being strong and breaking things.
I have a crude sense of humor.
I face problems head-on. A simple, direct solution is the best path to success.

from.name: Greater Good
from.desc: Our lot is to lay down our lives in defense of others. (Good)

from.name: Responsibility
from.desc: I do what I must and obey just authority. (Lawful)

from.name: Independence
from.desc: When people follow orders blindly, they embrace a kind of tyranny. (Chaotic)

from.name: Might
from.desc: In life as in war, the stronger force wins. (Evil)

from.name: Live and Let Live
from.desc: Ideals aren’t worth killing over or going to war for. (Neutral)

from.name: Nation
from.desc: My city, nation, or people are all that matter. (Any)

from.text: I would still lay down my life for the people I served with.
Someone saved my life on the battlefield. To this day, I will never leave a friend behind.
My honor is my life.
I’ll never forget the crushing defeat my company suffered—or the enemies who dealt it.
Those who fight beside me are those worth dying for.
I fight for those who cannot fight for themselves.
The monstrous enemy we faced in battle still leaves me shaking with fear.
I have little respect for anyone who is not a proven warrior.
I made a terrible mistake in battle that cost many lives—and I’ll do anything to keep that secret.
My hatred of my enemies is blind and unreasoning.
I obey the law, even when it causes misery.
I’d rather eat my armor than admit when I’m wrong.

backgrounds.name: Urban Bounty Hunter
backgrounds.description:
Before you became an adventurer, your life was already full of conflict and excitement, because you made a living tracking down people for pay. Unlike some people who collect bounties, though, you aren't a savage who follows quarry into or through the wilderness. You're involved in a lucrative trade, in the place where you live, that routinely tests your skills and survival instincts. What's more, you aren't alone, as a bounty hunter in the wild would be: you routinely interact with both the criminal subculture and other bounty hunters, maintaining contacts in both areas to help you succeed.

You might be a cunning thief-catcher, prowling the rooftops to catch one of the myriad burglars of the city. Perhaps you are someone who has your ear to the street, aware of the doings of thieves' guilds and street gangs. You might be a "velvet mask" bounty hunter, one who blends in with high society and noble circles in order to catch the criminals that prey on the rich, whether pickpockets or con artists. The community where you plied your trade might have been one of Faerûn's great metropolises, such as Waterdeep or Baldur's Gate, or a less populous location, perhaps Luskan or Yartar—any place that's large enough to have a steady supply of potential quarries.

As a member of an adventuring party, you might find it more difficult to pursue a personal agenda that doesn't fit with the group's objectives—but on the other hand, you can take down much more formidable targets with the help of your companions.

equipment: A set of clothes appropriate to your duties

features.name: Ear to the Ground
features.desc: You are in frequent contact with people in the segment of society that your chosen quarries move through. These people might be associated with the criminal underworld, the rough-and-tumble folk of the streets, or members of high society. This connection comes in the form of a contact in any city you visit, a person who provides information about the people and places of the local area.

choices.prompt: Choose 2 tool proficiencies (any combination of: one gaming set, one musical instrument, thieves' tools)
Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: I always have a plan for what to do when things go wrong.
I am always calm, no matter what the situation. I never raise my voice or let my emotions control me.
The first thing I do in a new place is note the locations of everything valuable—or where such things could be hidden.
I would rather make a new friend than a new enemy.
I am incredibly slow to trust. Those who seem the fairest often have the most to hide.
I don't pay attention to the risks in a situation. Never tell me the odds.
The best way to get me to do something is to tell me I can't do it.
I blow up at the slightest insult.

meta.note: SCAG: Suggested characteristics use the Criminal background tables as a basis.

from.name: Honor
from.desc: I don't steal from others in the trade. (Lawful)

from.name: Freedom
from.desc: Chains are meant to be broken, as are those who would forge them. (Chaotic)

from.name: Charity
from.desc: I steal from the wealthy so that I can help people in need. (Good)

from.name: Greed
from.desc: I will do whatever it takes to become wealthy. (Evil)

from.name: People
from.desc: I'm loyal to my friends, not to any ideals, and everyone else can take a trip down the Styx for all I care. (Neutral)

from.name: Redemption
from.desc: There's a spark of good in everyone. (Good)

meta.note: SCAG: Suggested characteristics use the Criminal background tables as a basis.

from.text: I'm trying to pay off an old debt I owe to a generous benefactor.
My ill-gotten gains go to support my family.
Something important was taken from me, and I aim to steal it back.
I will become the greatest thief that ever lived.
I'm guilty of a terrible crime. I hope I can redeem myself for it.
Someone I loved died because of a mistake I made. That will never happen again.

meta.note: SCAG: Suggested characteristics use the Criminal background tables as a basis.

from.text: When I see something valuable, I can't think about anything but how to steal it.
When faced with a choice between money and my friends, I usually choose the money.
If there's a plan, I'll forget it. If I don't forget it, I'll ignore it.
I have a "tell" that reveals when I'm lying.
I turn tail and run when things look bad.
An innocent person is in prison for a crime that I committed. I'm okay with that.

meta.note: SCAG: Suggested characteristics use the Criminal background tables as a basis.

backgrounds.name: Urchin
backgrounds.description:
You grew up on the streets alone, orphaned, and poor. You had no one to watch over you or to provide for you, so you learned to provide for yourself. You fought for food and kept a constant watch out for other desperate souls who might steal from you. You slept on rooftops and in alleyways, exposed to the elements, and endured sickness and starvation. You survived despite all odds, and did so through cunning, strength, speed, or some combination of each.

You begin your adventuring career with enough money to live modestly but securely for at least ten days. How did you come by that money? What allowed you to break free of your desperate circumstances and embark on a better life?

equipment: A map of the city you grew up in
A token to remember your parents by

features.name: City Secrets
features.desc: You know the secret patterns and flow to cities and can find passages through the urban sprawl that others would miss. When you are not in combat, you (and companions you lead) can travel between any two locations in the city twice as fast as your speed would normally allow.

choices.prompt: Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: I hide scraps of food and trinkets away in my pockets.
I ask a lot of questions.
I like to squeeze into small places where no one else can get to me.
I sleep with my back to a wall or tree, with everything I own wrapped in a bundle in my arms.
I eat like a pig and have bad manners.
I think anyone who's nice to me is hiding evil intent.
I don't like to bathe.
I bluntly say what other people are hinting at or hiding.

from.name: Respect
from.desc: All people, rich or poor, deserve respect. (Good)

from.name: Community
from.desc: We have to take care of each other, because no one else is going to do it. (Lawful)

from.name: Change
from.desc: The low are lifted up, and the high and mighty are brought down. Change is the nature of things. (Chaotic)

from.name: Retribution
from.desc: The rich need to be shown what life and death are like in the gutters. (Evil)

from.name: People
from.desc: I help the people who help me—that's what keeps us alive. (Neutral)

from.name: Aspiration
from.desc: I'm going to prove that I'm worthy of a better life. (Any)

from.text: My town or city is my home, and I'll fight to defend it.
I sponsor an orphanage to keep others from enduring what I was forced to endure.
I owe my survival to another urchin who taught me to live on the streets.
I owe a debt I can never repay to the person who took pity on me.
I escaped my life of poverty by robbing an important person, and I'm wanted for it.
No one else should have to endure the hardships I've been through.
If I'm outnumbered, I will run away from a fight.
Gold seems like a lot of money to me, and I'll do just about anything for more of it.
I will never fully trust anyone other than myself.
I'd rather kill someone in their sleep than fight fair.
It's not stealing if I need it more than someone else.
People who can't take care of themselves get what they deserve.

backgrounds.name: Uthgardt Tribe Member
backgrounds.description:
Though you might have only recently arrived in civilized lands, you are no stranger to the values of cooperation and group effort when striving for supremacy. You learned these principles, and much more, as a member of an Uthgardt tribe.

Your people have always tried to hold to the old ways. Tradition and taboo have kept the Uthgardt strong while the kingdoms of others have collapsed into chaos and ruin. But for the last few generations, some bands among the tribes were tempted to settle, make peace, trade, and even to build towns. Perhaps this is why Uthgar chose to raise up the totems among the people as living embodiments of his power.

You might have grown up among those who settled and were later cast adrift when the tribe returned to the old ways, or you might come from a fiercely traditional band and now seek to bring glory to your tribe through great deeds as an adventurer.

equipment: A totemic token or tattoos marking your loyalty to Uthgar and your tribal totem

features.name: Uthgardt Heritage
features.desc: You have an excellent knowledge of your tribe’s territory and the wider North. When you forage in wilderness areas, you find twice as much food and water as you normally would. In addition, you can call upon the hospitality of your tribe and allied peoples, often including druid circles, nomadic elves, the Harpers, and priesthoods of the gods of the First Circle.

choices.prompt: Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: I’m driven by a wanderlust that led me away from home.
I watch over my friends as if they were a litter of newborn pups.
I once ran twenty-five miles without stopping to warn my people of danger.
I have a lesson for every situation, drawn from the ways of the wild.
I place no stock in the comforts of civilization.
I feel far more comfortable under the open sky than beneath a roof.
I trust animals more than people.
I was raised to honor strength, endurance, and tradition.

from.name: Honor
from.desc: If I dishonor myself, I dishonor my whole tribe. (Lawful)

from.name: Might
from.desc: The strongest are meant to rule. (Evil)

from.name: Nature
from.desc: The spirits of the land and the old ways must be preserved. (Neutral)

from.name: Glory
from.desc: I must earn renown for myself and my tribe. (Any)

from.name: Greater Good
from.desc: My strength is meant to protect my people. (Good)

from.text: My tribe is my family, even if I walk far from them.
I will defend the ancestral mound sacred to my people.
I seek to restore my tribe’s honor or lost glory.
Uthgar’s will guides my actions, whether I understand it or not.
I will prove that my people are not weak or forgotten.
I am slow to trust anyone outside my people.
I have little patience for the laws and customs of civilization.
I answer insults with violence.
I believe weakness deserves no mercy.
I cling too tightly to tradition, even when it causes harm.

backgrounds.name: Waterdhavian Noble
backgrounds.description:
You are a scion of one of the great noble families of Waterdeep. Human families who jealously guard their privilege and place in the City of Splendors, Waterdhavian nobles have a reputation across Faerûn for being eccentric, spoiled, venal, and—above all else—rich.

Whether you embody this reputation or defy it, your surname carries weight. People expect things of you when they know who your family is. Your reasons for adventuring are almost certainly tied to your lineage: perhaps you are the black sheep of the family, or perhaps you adventure to enhance your house’s prestige and secure its legacy.

Work with your DM to determine your noble house. Waterdeep boasts dozens of lineages, each with its own alliances, schemes, and ambitions. You might be a central heir or a distant cousin—either way, your bloodline matters.

equipment: A skin of fine zzar or wine

features.name: Kept in Style
features.desc:
While you are in Waterdeep or elsewhere in the North, your family sees to your everyday needs. Your name and signet are sufficient to cover most routine expenses. Inns, taverns, and festhalls are willing to extend you credit and send an accounting to your family’s estate in Waterdeep.

This benefit allows you to maintain a comfortable lifestyle without paying its normal cost, or reduces the cost of a wealthy or aristocratic lifestyle by 2 gp per day. This benefit represents a line of credit, not actual income.

choices.prompt: Choose 2 personality traits (or write your own)

from.text: My eloquent flattery makes everyone I talk to feel like the most wonderful and important person in the world.
The common folk love me for my kindness and generosity.
No one could doubt by looking at my regal bearing that I am a cut above the unwashed masses.
I take great pains to always look my best and follow the latest fashions.
I don’t like to get my hands dirty, and I won’t be caught dead in unsuitable accommodations.
Despite my noble birth, I do not place myself above other folk. We all have the same blood.

from.name: Respect
from.desc: Respect is due to me because of my position, but all people deserve dignity. (Good)

from.name: Responsibility
from.desc: It is my duty to respect the authority of those above me and care for those below me. (Lawful)

from.name: Independence
from.desc: I must prove that I can handle myself without the coddling of my family. (Chaotic)

from.name: Power
from.desc: If I can attain more power, no one will tell me what to do. (Evil)

from.name: Family
from.desc: Blood runs thicker than water. (Any)

from.name: Noble Obligation
from.desc: It is my duty to protect and care for the people beneath me. (Good)

from.text: I will face any challenge to win the approval of my family.
My house’s alliance with another noble family must be sustained at all costs.
Nothing is more important than the honor of my family.
I am in love with an heir from a rival house.
My loyalty to my sovereign or city-state is unwavering.
The downfall of a rival house would secure my family’s future.
I secretly believe everyone beneath me is expendable.
I hide a truly scandalous secret that could ruin my family.
I have an insatiable desire for luxury and status.
I underestimate people who lack noble blood.
I am obsessed with maintaining appearances, no matter the cost.
I am ruled by the expectations placed upon me.

backgrounds.name: Witchlight Hand
backgrounds.description:
You crept into the Witchlight Carnival as a child or youth and never looked back, earning a place among those who work behind the scenes to keep the carnival in business. As a hand, you work hard and party hard. The carnival has borne you to many fantastic worlds, circling back around to your home world once every eight years, but you know almost nothing about these worlds because you spend all your time in the carnival. You know the other hands well, but the carnival’s owners—Mister Witch and Mister Light—remain mysterious to you even after all these years.

Maybe you joined the carnival to escape a dismal life at home, or maybe you were enchanted by the idea of visiting new places or the dream of becoming one of the carnival’s star attractions. Now that you’re older, carnival life has lost some of its appeal. The daily routine has grown tedious, the cyclical nature of the carnival’s journey monotonous. The carnival no longer fills your heart with a sense of wonder. Perhaps greater adventures await you beyond the carnival’s gates.

features.name: Carnival Fixture
features.desc: The Witchlight Carnival provides you with free, modest lodging and food. In addition, you may wander about the carnival and partake of its many wonders at no cost to you, provided you don’t disrupt its shows or cause any other trouble.

features.name: Carnival Companion
features.desc: Over the years, you have earned the friendship of another carnival fixture. This companion hangs around you while you’re in the carnival but won’t voluntarily leave it. Work with your DM to flesh out this relationship.

choices.prompt: Choose or roll for your Carnival Companion

from.text: Old, cantankerous Witchlight hand
